threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 'Shoreditch Effect' is just a trendy art show, not a reason to raise rent prices across Stoke Newington.
On the 75 last night, a lad argued passionately with his kebab about the meaning of life. The bus driver just chuckled and kept driving.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I caught the scent of freshly baked lahmacun wafting from the corner bakery at 3 AM last weekend. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a currywurst at 4 AM and still hear someone passionately debating the latest techno festival during your midnight snack.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively know to avoid BART delays and plan their evenings around it, not just complain about it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with a pet parrot on the 19 Polk—parrot was squawking about the weather while everyone else was glued to their phones. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just grab a coffee at 4 PM without realizing most locals are on the 'tapa' circuit by then, not sitting in cafés.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their overly enthusiastic 'Hola!' from a distance, while locals stick to discreet nods and brief smiles.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'going for a pint' means a casual evening; locals know it's a serious commitment, especially with the pub closing times around here.
Last night on the 59, a lad was singing his own rap while the driver tried to keep a straight face. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing football in the street, while an older man sold fresh pretzels from his bicycle nearby. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a U-Bahn station that doubles as a club on weekends—get your techno fix without leaving the tracks.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their patience at the BART delays. They quietly calculate their next move instead of panicking.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 38 this morning, clearly late to a wedding. He stood out among the usual morning hustle like a misplaced wedding cake.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think 'Gràcia' is just about Gaudí. They miss the hidden art squats and the locals in the squares, sipping vermut and sharing stories.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their reluctance to speak Catalan, even when everyone around is using it like it’s second nature.
